_meta.description: Soft memory stores user preferences that influence writing style but are not hard constraints. These are learned patterns that can be overridden by explicit user instructions.
_meta.usage: This file is loaded in Phase A (GATHER) as a runtime asset. Preferences here guide style choices (e.g., voice, connectors, equation format) but do not override explicit user instructions in the current session.
_meta.maintenance: Entries are updated when consistent user preferences are observed across sessions. Changes are additive; old preferences are kept but can be marked as deprecated.

target_word_count.note: Dense paragraphs, one core idea each. Extracted from own paper set.

paragraph_preferences.opening_sentence_note: Each paragraph opens with a sentence stating its purpose, e.g., 'The [COMPONENT] module is designed to [PURPOSE].'
paragraph_preferences.closing_sentence_note: No single dominant pattern. Sometimes transition, sometimes summary, sometimes equation reference.
paragraph_preferences.internal_flow_note: Purpose/context -> method/approach -> technical detail/formulation (loose 3-beat, not rigid)

first_person_en.note: All 5 own papers use zero instances of 'we'. Use 'this study' / 'this paper' instead.

equation_preferences.post_where_note: Interpretation typically comes in the next paragraph, not immediately after the where block.

section_ordering_preference.note: This section is deprecated. Use methodology_patterns.yml for pattern selection instead. Do not rely on a single invariant order.

humanizer_preference.note: Always ask before applying humanizer. User has not expressed a default preference yet.

technical_verb_preferences.note: utilize and leverage appear in own papers (Papers 04-05), so they are acceptable.
